channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Was the Iran war the final blow in the collapse of Spirit Airlines?
item.description: The shutdown of the budget airline after 34 years has left 17,000 staff members unemployed.

item.title: OPEC+ announces symbolic oil output rise during Strait of Hormuz closure
item.description: Seven major producers will add 188,000 barrels per day to their total production quota for June, OPEC+ says.

item.title: Israel approves purchase of fighter jets from the US
item.description: Purchase includes two new combat squadrons of F-35 and F-15IA aircraft from Lockheed Martin and Boeing.

item.title: US military says two service members taking part in Morocco drills missing
item.description: AFRICOM says search and rescue operations under way in southwestern Morocco during African Lion exercise.

item.title: Taiwan’s leader visits Eswatini despite pressure from China to block trip
item.description: Taiwan’s leader William Lai Ching-te is visiting Eswatini, the only country in Africa that recognises Taiwan.

item.title: Two women die in attempted Channel crossing from France to UK
item.description: Women die after boat carrying 82 people has engine trouble and runs aground near Calais, French authorities say.

item.title: Gaza aid flotilla activists appear in Israeli court after abduction
item.description: Court extends activists’ detention by two days for further interrogation, rights group tells Al Jazeera.

item.title: LIVE: Manchester United vs Liverpool – Premier League
item.description: Follow the build-up, analysis and live text commentary of the game as United host rivals Liverpool at Old Trafford.

item.title: Who is Cherie DeVaux, the first female trainer to win the Kentucky Derby?
item.description: DeVaux-trained Golden Tempo beats the odds to give jockey Jose Ortiz a win in the derby for the first time.

item.title: What’s Iran’s 14-point proposal to end the war? And will Trump accept it?
item.description: Trump is reviewing Iran's latest proposal to end the war, but the mistrust between the two sides is a big obstacle.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz blockade and other major naval sieges in modern times
item.description: Naval blockades are among the oldest weapons of war, using sea control to cut supplies and force an enemy’s surrender.

item.title: Hospitals in Lebanon overwhelmed with patients injured by Israeli attacks
item.description: Hospitals in Lebanon overwhelmed with patients injured by Israeli attacks

item.title: After Maduro
item.description: Keeping the Revolution alive - After Maduro’s capture, we followed the people fighting to bring him back

item.title: Ben-Gvir celebrates birthday with cake decorated with death penalty noose
item.description: Ben-Gvir celebrated his 50th birthday with a cake referencing the newly-passed death penalty for Palestinian detainees.

item.title: Ukraine drone attack hits Russian Baltic port
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said attack was 'successful destruction' of the port, as Russia warns of oil price rises.

item.title: Are Somali pirates on a comeback in 2026?
item.description: Somali pirates peaked in 2010, vanished by 2013, and are back in 2026.

item.title: Israel issues new forced displacement orders in southern Lebanon
item.description: Warning covers more than 10 villages and towns, including several in Nabatieh district that lie north of Litani River.

item.title: Gretchen Walsh lowers own 100m butterfly world record for fourth time
item.description: Walsh won in 54.33 seconds at the Fort Lauderdale Open in Florida, where she set her previous world record of 54.60s.

item.title: The World Cup & Passport privilege
item.description: With US President Donald Trump's strict immigration policies, some fans may never make it past the American border

item.title: Austrian police arrest man after discovery of rat poison in baby food
item.description: Arrest of 39-year-old comes after Swiss company HiPP last month issued product recall over tainted baby food jars.

item.title: Japan’s Inoue beats Nakatani to retain undisputed super bantamweight title
item.description: Naoya Inoue wins 116-112, 115-113, 116-112 in a fight billed as the biggest boxing match in ​Japanese history.

item.title: South Korea holds pro-Palestine protest in solidarity with Gaza
item.description: Protesters in South Korea’s capital, Seoul, were in solidarity with Palestinians in Gaza enduring genocidal attacks.

item.title: Man charged with murder of five-year-old Australian Indigenous girl
item.description: The man was also charged with two other offences and is to appear ​in court in Darwin on Tuesday, police said.

item.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 65 as Trump reviews new plan to end war?
item.description: Iran has sent the US a new 14-point proposal to end the war.

item.title: China blocks US sanctions against five ‘teapot’ refineries
item.description: Ministry of Commerce says sanctions against refineries accused of importing Iranian oil violate international law.
